purpose: Central global-state store for the entire dashboard. Implements a useReducer-based store split into two contexts — AppContext (state) and DispatchContext (dispatch) — provided together by AppProvider. Owns ~80 reducer cases covering: per-dashboard chat state (claude* slice), per-chat-agent chat state (chatClaude* slice), dashboard list/names/states, IDE workspaces/files/file-trees/search/debug/diagnostics, Git Manager repos/branches/log/diff, live-preview state, modals, view routing, permission requests, queue, and unread counts. Implements the surface-aware slice pattern (SLICE_FIELDS.code vs SLICE_FIELDS.chat) so the same CLAUDE_* action types route to either claudeMessages or chatClaudeMessages based on action.surface. Persists chat tabs, IDE workspaces, git repos, chat-tab messages, and per-tab Claude transcripts to localStorage with debounced writes (500ms via schedulePersist).
gotchas: Two contexts (AppContext + DispatchContext) — always import both individually via useAppState() and useDispatch(); do NOT useContext(AppContext) directly.
AppContext re-renders on EVERY state change — every consumer re-renders unless wrapped in React.memo. The split into DispatchContext means components that only dispatch (no read) never re-render.

SLICE_FIELDS.code vs SLICE_FIELDS.chat is the foundation of the dual chat shell — passing wrong action.surface silently writes to the wrong slice. Backward-compat: missing action.surface defaults to 'code' via fieldsFor().
appReducer wraps appReducerCore and triggers schedulePersist for CLAUDE_PERSIST_ACTIONS only — appending a new persist-worthy action type requires adding it to CLAUDE_PERSIST_ACTIONS set.

schedulePersist uses a single module-level persistTimer — concurrent dispatches collapse into one write (last-write-wins), which is desirable for streaming deltas but means you can lose intermediate states on quick crash.
trimMessages caps each tab at MAX_CHAT_MESSAGES (200) and inserts a 'X older messages trimmed' system notice — long-running chats will show this notice once cap is hit.

loadSavedMessages does a one-time migration from legacy 'synapse-claude-messages' key to dashboard1 default tab — only runs when (!tabId || tabId==='default') && (!dashboardId || dashboardId==='dashboard1').
CLAUDE_WELCOME_MSG is reused as fallback in many places — comparing message.id === 'welcome' is a reliable way to detect 'no real messages yet'.
SWITCH_DASHBOARD stashes prev dashboard's chat state, restores target's — uses cachedProgress/cachedLogs from allDashboardProgress/allDashboardLogs to avoid 'pending' flicker during async re-fetch.
chatTabContextId(tab) returns 'chat-agent-' + tab.agentHex when agentHex exists, otherwise tab.id — chat-agent backfill effect (in AppProvider) allocates agentHex for legacy tabs lacking one.
REMOVE_DASHBOARD has full cleanup logic — purges localStorage keys, stash, tabs, names, unread; if the removed dashboard was active, auto-switches to dashboardList[0] with full state restore.
IDE_OPEN_FILE de-dupes by path: if same path is already open it just switches active file; new files get id of Date.now() + random — IDs are NOT deterministic.
GIT_NAVIGATE_TO_FILE doubles as 'open repo if not open' AND 'switch view to git AND highlight file' — single action, multi-effect.
previewEditHistory is capped at 100 entries — silently drops oldest via slice(-100).
Persistence of chatClaudeMessages happens both inside reducer cases (synchronous localStorage.setItem on tab switches) AND via debounced schedulePersist for streaming — two paths.
patterns: Surface-aware slice routing: action.surface ('code'|'chat') + fieldsFor(surface) + SLICE_FIELDS lookup table — adopt this pattern when adding any chat field.
Context split for performance: AppContext (state) + DispatchContext (dispatch) — components needing only dispatch import only useDispatch.
Action shape: { type: 'NAMESPACE_ACTION', ...payload } — payload keys vary per action; switch cases destructure them inline.
Persistence helpers (loadSaved*, save*) live in the file with try/catch around localStorage to handle quota/private-browsing — never let a localStorage failure crash the app.
Tab stash key pattern: dashboardId + ':' + tabId — used as both localStorage key suffix and in-memory tabStash key.
Initial state bootstrap reads localStorage synchronously at module-load time (savedWorkspaces, savedGitRepos, savedChatTabs, etc.) — first-render shows persisted state.
useRef(false) + useEffect for one-shot migrations (backfilledRef pattern) — ensures async migration runs exactly once across re-renders.
conventions: Action types are SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE namespaced: CLAUDE_*, IDE_*, GIT_*, DEBUG_*, DIAGNOSTICS_*, PREVIEW_*, CHAT_TAB_*.
Reducer returns new state via spread; every case immutably copies maps with { ...state.foo, [key]: val }.
localStorage keys all prefixed with 'synapse-' to avoid collisions with the host's other localStorage usage.
